page: Amazon Web Services r6g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates balanced multi-core performance alongside some single-core limitations. The instance achieves average ratings in multi-core processing, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24,284.19 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8,428.0. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 946.72 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 121.62 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-threaded performance is weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 929.0 and a poor rating in single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Database operations and Redis SET performance remain steady in the average tier, with Redis handling 18.82 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 Graviton architecture offers a resource-dense profile for memory-bound workloads without the premium associated with traditional x86 memory-optimized instances. The absence of local storage and specialized GPU hardware positions this instance as a dedicated host for memory-intensive applications that do not require local high-speed scratch space. It is highly suited for workloads such as distributed databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, open-source database operations, and general-purpose memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 5 Gbps baseline network connection. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24,284.19 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8,428.0. Memory read bandwidth reaches 946.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks, though single-core performance is relatively weak. Qualitatively, the custom ARM architecture provides a cost-effective alternative for memory-heavy applications compared to traditional x86 platforms. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as Redis caching, database operations, and medium-scale enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB of RAM, and a 5 Gbps network baseline, ideal for in-memory databases and caching.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixteen dedicated cores and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 2.5 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage or GPU hardware included.
Average multi-core performance with 18.82 million Redis SET operations per second.
Suited for in-memory caching, distributed databases, and memory-bound applications.
